mnemonics: FAMILY TREE (Think of a family hierarchy: Grandparent -> Parent -> Child, just like classes inheriting from one another in a multilevel hierarchy.)
keyPoints: A multilevel hierarchy is created when a class inherits from a derived class, making the new class a derived class of a derived class.
The 'super' keyword is used in a constructor to call the immediate parent class constructor.
A subclass inherits all non-private members (fields and methods) from all of its superclasses up the inheritance chain.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to call 'super()' in the child class constructor, which can lead to the parent class not being initialized correctly.
Creating overly deep and complex inheritance hierarchies that are difficult to understand and maintain, when composition might be a better choice.
